parse_warnings.message: Article 'In this Allstacks whitepaper, Jim Grundner maps the AI-driven PM lifecycle, how product teams can move faster, and how to practically create context-aware requirements for AI agents.' has no reference number

articles.title: MOVING AWAY FROM TAILWIND, AND LEARNING TO STRUCTURE MY CSS
articles.summary: A transition away from Tailwind CSS means adopting a workflow centered on semantic HTML and native CSS, and organizing code into isolated component files while still using Tailwind-inspired concepts like color palettes. Modern CSS features such as nesting and grid layouts are great for clean, responsive code organization.

articles.title: CONTEXT PRUNING: CUT LLM TOKENS WITHOUT LOSING QUALITY
articles.summary: Context pruning is a technique that removes low-value elements from an LLM's input, such as tokens or passages, to reduce costs and improve LLM output, often resulting in up to 20x compression and faster latency. It works by mitigating issues like "lost in the middle" effects common in long context windows, but must be applied carefully as it can negatively impact structured data or multi-turn dialogue.

articles.title: I DON'T THINK AI WILL MAKE YOUR PROCESSES GO FASTER
articles.summary: Organizations often have unrealistic expectations that AI will automatically resolve bottlenecks and speed up complex business processes. Delays in areas like software development typically arise from vague feature requests and incomplete documentation rather than a lack of technical execution speed. This means AI can't really help in these cases.

articles.title: THE CTF SCENE IS DEAD
articles.summary: Frontier AI models have disrupted the open Capture The Flag (CTF) format by automating the reasoning and technical tasks that once defined human skill. These competitions have evolved into a test of computational orchestration and financial investment, making traditional scoreboards an inaccurate measure of individual security expertise.

articles.title: YOUR PRODUCT MANAGEMENT PRACTICES ARE KILLING AI ROI
articles.summary: Engineering accelerated, but product management didn't. It's 10-100x cheaper to fix the requirements than bad code in review or production.

articles.title: In this Allstacks whitepaper, Jim Grundner maps the AI-driven PM lifecycle, how product teams can move faster, and how to practically create context-aware requirements for AI agents.
SEMBLE
articles.summary: Semble is a high-performance code search library designed to help AI agents find specific code snippets via natural language, improving efficiency by using approximately 98% fewer tokens while maintaining quality. Running entirely on a CPU with no need for external hardware, this system provides near-instant indexing and sub-millisecond responses and can be integrated through an MCP server, CLI, or Python API.

articles.title: ZEROSTACK
articles.summary: Zerostack is a lightweight and performant Rust-based coding agent that supports multiple AI providers and has a permission system, session management, and specialized tool integrations like Git worktrees and ACP.

articles.title: EVERY AI SUBSCRIPTION IS A TICKING TIME BOMB FOR ENTERPRISE
articles.summary: Major AI providers are currently subsidizing enterprise subscriptions at a loss to encourage widespread adoption and weave their technology into core business workflows. The emergence of resource-heavy agentic AI has made this financial gap worse, as the actual cost of compute often dwarfs the flat monthly fees companies are currently paying. As these labs move toward public offerings, the pressure to achieve profitability will inevitably trigger a transition to expensive, usage-based pricing models.

articles.title: HOW BRAZE'S CTO IS RETHINKING ENGINEERING FOR THE AGENTIC AREA
articles.summary: Braze shifted to AI-first engineering in a few months, with 60%+ of committed code now AI-generated. Adoption was driven by model quality (Claude Code, Opus 4.5) and a greenfield MCP server. Now the problem they're dealing with is inference cost, as engineers are spending tens or hundreds of dollars a day on using AI.

articles.title: AI DELIVERY IS CREATING NEW BOTTLENECKS
TEAMS ARE MOVING FASTER WITH AI, BUT GOVERNANCE, TECHNICAL COMPLEXITY, AND RISING ROI EXPECTATIONS ARE MAKING MEASURABLE DELIVERY HARDER. LEARN WHAT HIGH-PERFORMING TEAMS DO DIFFERENTLY
NATIVE ALL THE WAY, UNTIL YOU NEED TEXT
articles.summary: Native Apple development frameworks like SwiftUI and AppKit are often not enough for building complex, text-heavy chat interfaces, leading devs to favor web-based platforms like Electron for their better rich text rendering and out-of-the-box performance.

articles.title: AI IS TECHNOLOGY, NOT A PRODUCT
articles.summary: AI is an essential underlying technology that will eventually pervade all Apple devices rather than manifest as a standalone "killer product" that replaces the iPhone ecosystem.

articles.title: APPLE SILICON COSTS MORE THAN OPENROUTER
articles.summary: Running LLMs locally on Apple Silicon hardware is much more expensive and slower than using cloud services like OpenRouter due to high hardware depreciation costs compared to the price per million tokens.

articles.title: WHY I DON'T CHAIN EVERYTHING IN JAVASCRIPT ANYMORE
articles.summary: Transitioning from complex JavaScript method chains to explicit, step-by-step logic improves code readability, simplifies debugging, and avoids the performance costs of unnecessary data processing.
